text.title: Docker Compose (Infrastructure) Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the Docker Compose (Infrastructure) methodology. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Database-tier services MUST be on a network with 'internal: true'. Public app tier joins both networks. Without it the DB is reachable from any container that knows the IP.
rule.rationale: Database-tier services MUST be on a network with 'internal: true'. Public app tier joins both networks. Without it the DB is reachable from any container that knows the IP.

rule.statement: Every service sets deploy.resources.limits.cpus + memory. Without limits one container starves the host.
rule.rationale: Every service sets deploy.resources.limits.cpus + memory. Without limits one container starves the host.

rule.statement: Long-running services use restart: unless-stopped. 'always' overrides operator stop intent; 'no' loses services after reboot.
rule.rationale: Long-running services use restart: unless-stopped. 'always' overrides operator stop intent; 'no' loses services after reboot.

rule.statement: File is compose.yaml (not docker-compose.yml). Modern Compose discovers compose.yaml first.
rule.rationale: File is compose.yaml (not docker-compose.yml). Modern Compose discovers compose.yaml first.

rule.statement: Persistent data uses named volumes. Bind mounts are reserved for config files mounted read-only.
rule.rationale: Persistent data uses named volumes. Bind mounts are reserved for config files mounted read-only.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent's context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-docker-compose.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
